caveats.notes: Migrated from M-30 honest-residual list. Closed by running canonical-22 with COMPGEN_RUN_KERNELS=1 on a host with cuda hardware AND host_cpu, so the same region_signature observes >=2 distinct target_class values.
caveats.required_to_close: run same region_signature on >=2 target_class values (need cuda hardware on the audit host)

caveats.notes: CI cannot spawn a Claude Code session against the task pack; doing so would defeat the no-private-context goal of the audit. The greedy baseline is the contractual reproducibility floor; the fresh-Claude run is recorded as an operator evidence row with a quarterly cadence.
caveats.required_to_close: operator runs a fresh Claude Code session against the task pack and records the outcome via compgen.audit.fresh_agent_modes.record_manual_session_result every quarter

caveats.notes: M-37.13's honest fix replaced M-37.12's hand-picked combined tolerance with two layered checks: (1) STRUCTURAL — simulator must match a tile-K-aware reference bit-exactly; this is mathematically guaranteed by the simulator's design (it enforces the same K-accumulation order as the reference); (2) SEMANTIC — Higham's matmul accumulation bound (4 * K * eps * max|A| * max|B|) derived per-case from inputs, never silently widened. The load-bearing safety has shifted from real_lowering's selected_model_is_merlin_mlp_wide whitelist to recipe_gate's single_k_iter rule + M-12's two-layered honest checks. Under correct math the structural invariant always holds; a natural M-12 differential failure now requires either a real backend codegen producing genuinely-wrong arithmetic OR a simulator implementation bug. The three end-to-end tests that previously asserted natural M-12 failure on tiny_mlp now exercise the M-15B detector + emitter against a tampered status=fail report; the structural and semantic invariants are exercised directly via TestStructuralBitEqualityVsTileKReference and TestHighamSemanticBound.
caveats.required_to_close: ship a real backend execution path (Triton or accelerator codegen) whose differential against eager exhibits genuine numerical disagreement past Higham's bound, OR widen the canonical set with a model whose region produces a structural simulator-vs-reference mismatch

caveats.notes: MOSEK is an optional licensed numeric backend. When the repo-local mosek.lic is missing or its token cannot be obtained, the planner falls back to HiGHS. The HiGHS fallback is sufficient for the M-66D memory MILP formulation; quadratic / conic formulations remain blocked by HiGHS capability (see highs_milp_fallback_limited).
caveats.required_to_close: ship a host with MOSEK installed AND a valid license at <repo>/mosek.lic OR MOSEKLM_LICENSE_FILE

caveats.notes: M-68 emits a typed overlap_schedule.solved.json; consuming it inside the ASYNC glue + measuring a real CUDA differential requires CUDA hardware on the audit host. When CUDA is unavailable the glue-differential test is skipped honestly rather than faked green.
caveats.required_to_close: run the M-68 -> ASYNC glue pipeline on a host with CUDA hardware and record the differential

caveats.notes: HiGHS's MILP capability is narrower than MOSEK's; quadratic and conic constraints that MOSEK accepts are not supported under HiGHS. When such a formulation arrives and MOSEK is unavailable, the planner returns SolverStatus.BLOCKED with this caveat in the response, not a degraded greedy heuristic.
caveats.required_to_close: ship MOSEK with a valid license, OR add an osqp/clarabel backend that handles the quadratic/conic case

caveats.notes: M-31 shipped the real pass-card registry; M-33 ships the verification-certificate gate and the producer-side invalidation discipline. All three previously-placeholder negative controls are now real fault injection: MissingPassCard (M-31; build a registry with a card missing and ask the validator to resolve both ids), PreconditionViolation (M-33; declare a card with a required verification rung, emit no certificate, assert VerificationGateMissing fires — note the test parametrize records the actual class raised), StaleAnalysisAudit (M-33; pass mutates graph_dossier_v3 but only claims semantic_obligations as invalidated, transitive closure check raises UnannouncedInvalidation which subclasses StaleAnalysisAudit).
caveats.required_to_close: Resolved by M-33. A future M-34 will add per-pass IR-level precondition checking (currently the closest typed error is VerificationGateMissing) and consumer-side stale-read detection (currently producer-side only).
